# Mosey

> Mosey is a motion-gated podcast player for iOS and Android. Audio plays only
> while you walk, jog, or run — stop, and the episode pauses and waits.
> Episode length is measured in steps, not minutes. Launching August 2026.

Mosey ties the thing people crave (the next episode) to the thing they keep
meaning to do (move). The motion gate attaches movement to an existing listening
habit instead of asking people to build a new fitness routine.

## Key facts

- Category: podcast player / walking habit app
- Core mechanic: motion-gated playback — plays while moving and pauses when
  stopped, with a grace window for traffic lights and crosswalks
- Episodes are measured in steps; sessions generate Echoes, shareable generative
  art drawn from cadence as organic growth rings
- Privacy: no GPS, maps, routes, or location data; motion sensing is on-device
- Accessibility: Adaptive Mode calibrates the gate to different paces and
  movement styles
- Social feed and Listen Together group walks are post-launch roadmap features
- Pricing: free tier; Mosey Plus at $9.99/month or $49.99/year with a full trial
- Platforms: iOS and Android, built with Kotlin and Compose Multiplatform

## Pages

- [Home](https://heymosey.app/): what Mosey is and how the motion gate works
- [Build log](https://heymosey.app/log/): design, code, numbers, and mistakes on
  the road to the August 2026 launch

## Contact

- Website: https://heymosey.app/
